case.title: TAYLOR v. DRAINAGE DIST NO 56 OF EMMET COUNTY, IOWA

opinion.h: TAYLOR v. DRAINAGE DIST NO 56 OF EMMET COUNTY, IOWA , 244 U.S. 644 (1917)
EMMA TAYLOR,1 Administratrix of Frank Taylor, Deceased, and John P. Kirby, Plaintiffs in Error, v. DRAINAGE DISTRICT NUMBER FIFTY-SIX OF EMMET COUNTY, IOWA, et al. No. 178.
Supreme Court of the United States
opinion.p: Mr. Edgar A. Morling for plaintiffs in error.
Messrs. Hugh H. Obear, Charles A. Douglas, Thomas Ruffin, and James W. Morse for defendants in error.

Judgment affirmed with costs upon the authority of Huling v. Kaw Valley R. & Improv. Co. 130 U.S. 559, 32 L. Ed. 1045, 9 Sup. Ct. Rep. 603; Winona & St. P. Land Co. v. Minnesota, 159 U.S. 540, 40 L. Ed. 252, 16 Sup. Ct. Rep. 88; Leigh v. Green, 193 U.S. 79, 48 L. Ed. 623, 24 Sup. Ct. Rep. 390; Ballard v. Hunter, 204 U.S. 241, 261, 262 S., 51 L. Ed. 461, 474, 475, 27 Sup. Ct. Rep. 261; American Land Co. v. Zeiss, 219 U.S. 47, 55 L. Ed. 82, 31 Sup. Ct. Rep. 200.

f.p: Death of Frank Taylor, one of the plaintiffs in error herein, suggested, and the appearance of Emma Taylor, administratrix, as a party plaintiff in error, filed and entered November 13, 1916, on motion of Mr. Hugh H. Obear, in behalf of counsel for the plaintiffs in error.[ Taylor v. Drainage Dist No 56 of Emmet County, Iowa 244 U.S. 644 (1917) ]
